nodes.name: Warpath Protocol Failure
nodes.brief: CONCORD's war-response system disabled by cyberattack of unprecedented complexity. Within minutes, four-empire cross-incursions in highsec. Single message left: 'Warpath, a gift dear empires. After all, toys are meant to be played with.' Origin unknown.
nodes.master_summary: The structural fracture of the YC128 cycle. CONCORD's war-response automation collapsed under a precise cyberattack, allowing all four empires to invade each other's high-sec for the first time since the Yulai event of YC110. Marks the moment the high-sec social contract stopped being a structural guarantee and became a narrative one. Origin remains unattributed; the message text reads as Guristas-flavored.

nodes.name: Jita Gate Incident
nodes.brief: All seven stargates damaged simultaneously by hyperspace fractures matching those seen in fractured fields. Death toll in thousands. Mechanism: manipulation of Drifter Hyperspace Network data-transfer layer. Ongoing radiation visible from nearby systems.
nodes.master_summary: The triggering catastrophe. Hyperspace fractures around all seven gates killed thousands and turned Jita into a politically untenable hub for the Caldari State. Crucially, the mechanism — manipulation of Drifter Hyperspace Network data layers — is only accessible to the five wormhole occupiers. That technical fact contradicts the Caldari accusation against the Gallente and forces the conspiracy outward toward Amarr / EDENCOM / Triglavian co-conspirators.

nodes.name: Silphy Summoned to New Caldari
nodes.brief: Intaki Syndicate Chair summoned for inquest into Syndicate involvement in gate disaster. Three days before her arrest.
nodes.master_summary: The diplomatic on-ramp to Silphy en Diabel's arrest. Three days before her detention, the Caldari summoned her to New Caldari for inquest. The summons signaled that the Syndicate–Caldari constellation deal had collapsed and gave the State the procedural pretext to detain a foreign head of state.

nodes.name: Arrest of Silphy en Diabel
nodes.brief: Detained in New Caldari by Lai Dai Protection Services. Two Syndicate agents convicted in March named her under plea agreement.
nodes.master_summary: The pivot point between Jita-gate and the Syndicate conquest. Two Syndicate agents convicted in March named Silphy under plea, giving the Caldari Navy the legal cover to detain her in New Caldari and frame the broader Syndicate as compromised. Without this arrest, the conquest framing would not have held diplomatically.

nodes.name: Caldari Conquest of Syndicate
nodes.brief: Caldari Navy declared sovereignty over all 106 Syndicate systems. Most local authorities surrendered with limited fire exchange. First forcible empire annexation of nullsec in modern history.
nodes.master_summary: The first forcible empire annexation of nullsec in modern New Eden history. Caldari Navy seized 106 systems against minimal resistance, framed as retaliation for Silphy's role in Jita-gate. Strategically eliminates the buffer state Lai Dai had been working through; structurally hands Mountain+Ocean factions the win they couldn't get politically.

nodes.name: Syndicate Capital Breakout
nodes.brief: Three escort intercept windows in highsec constellations Pelille / Bayet / Orvolle. Capsuleer bounty-paid kills on Syndicate fugitive capitals using captured highsec-cyno tech.
nodes.master_summary: The capsuleer-facing climax of the conquest. Three highsec constellation windows allow the Syndicate fugitive capitals — using captured high-sec cyno tech inherited from the YC123 dread assaults — to attempt escape. CCP-built escort intercept event, but lore-significant because the same tech links forward to next month's expansion.

nodes.name: Capsuleer Day XXIII — Warpath
nodes.brief: Live event encompassing Warpath cross-empire incursions, Syndicate conquest, capital breakout, capital-doomsday site rollout. Run-up to Fanfest YC128.
nodes.master_summary: The framing event that bundles the four-empire incursion arc, the Syndicate conquest, and the capital breakout into a single live-narrative payload. Run-up to Fanfest YC128, where the Military Campaigns expansion will use this arc as its launch pad.

nodes.name: Capital Support / Doomsday Site Rollout
nodes.brief: PvE site requiring cap-booster delivery to allied Titan; success triggers doomsday on hostile Titan.
nodes.master_summary: Patch-note PvE site dropping mid-event. Capsuleers deliver cap-boosters to an allied Titan to trigger a doomsday on a hostile Titan. Mechanically novel; lore-wise reinforces that empire navies can field supercapital force in highsec under the new high-sec cyno regime.

nodes.name: Gallente Presidential Election YC128
nodes.brief: Alix Moreau (Autonomist) won 34.8% — narrowly beat Soraya Roden (Progressive). Lucas Tenzin third. One of the closest elections on record.
nodes.master_summary: Moreau's narrow win over Roden inverted the expected hawkish response to Caldari escalation. The Federation elected a reformer at the exact moment State aggression peaked, structurally weakening the Federation military response and giving Mountain/Ocean an opening they wouldn't have had under a Roden presidency.

nodes.name: Drifter Wormhole Summit
nodes.brief: Held at Jita IV-4 (YC126). Five wormhole occupiers promoted destructive testing of the Drifter hyperspace network.
nodes.master_summary: The YC126 summit at Jita IV-4 where the five wormhole occupiers (Amarr, Caldari, EDENCOM, Minmatar, Triglavian) jointly committed to destructive testing of the Drifter hyperspace network. Direct ancestor of the Jita-gate weaponization — they collectively published the cookbook.

nodes.name: AMP Raises Drifter Experiment Warnings
nodes.brief: Anoyus Mapping Project published findings of erratic hyperspace telemetry inside Drifter wormholes. Founder Rio Talifo flagged risk of spillover to known space. Akimaka Saraki dismissed AMP as Gallente puppet.
nodes.master_summary: Rio Talifo and the Anoyus Mapping Project went public with concrete telemetry showing Drifter wormhole instability spilling into known space. Saraki dismissed it as Federation propaganda. Vindicated by Jita-gate; vindicated again by the Tanu II / Catalyst-era prismatic sites.

nodes.name: Drifter Crisis (final stages)
nodes.brief: Five factions pushed Drifters out of wormhole space; took possession of Drifter pillar formations. Triggered the destructive-testing era.
nodes.master_summary: The YC125 events that put the five empire-and-collective occupiers in possession of Drifter pillar formations. Without this crisis there is no occupier list, no destructive-testing summit, and no Jita-gate. Establishes the access list that lawyers the Caldari accusation against Gallente.

nodes.name: Starkmanir Liberation (Imperian Age)
nodes.brief: Elder Fleet revealed Nefantar (Ammatar) had hidden surviving Starkmanir from Amarr eradication. Starkmanir restored as Minmatar tribe.
nodes.master_summary: The YC110 Elder Fleet operation that liberated the Starkmanir from the Ammatar Mandate. Forms the historical bookend to the Mil-Campaign #2 region — the previous time CONCORD's framework failed to constrain inter-empire violence over the Mandate.

nodes.name: YC123 Dread Assaults (Tanu / Junfaa)
nodes.brief: Mercenary fleets used illegal high-sec-authorized cyno beacons to land dread assaults at Tanu and Junfaa. Established canonical existence of empire-circumventing cyno tech. Tanu II is the capital of the Ammatar Mandate — the same site Ash predicts as Mil-Campaign #2 zone.
nodes.master_summary: The canonical precedent for high-sec-authorized illegal cyno beacons. Mercenary fleets used them to land dread assaults at Tanu and Junfaa. The same tech returns five years later for the Syndicate breakout — the inheritance is the load-bearing connection.

nodes.name: CONCORD System Failure YC110
nodes.brief: Caused destruction of CONCORD's Bureau station in Yulai. General blackout. Cited as the prior precedent for systemic CONCORD failure (Warpath being more targeted).
nodes.master_summary: The previous time CONCORD broke. Bureau station destroyed at Yulai during a system-wide blackout. Cited explicitly in Warpath-era reporting as the comparison case: Yulai was a general failure, Warpath is targeted.

nodes.name: Federation Seizure of Intaki
nodes.brief: Federation aggressively removed Intaki + two adjacent constellations from FW war zone, made them full Federation property. Triggered the 'Uprising' arc and the original Caldari–Intaki Syndicate constellation deal.
nodes.master_summary: The YC124 Federation seizure of Intaki + two adjacent constellations. Triggered the Caldari–Intaki Syndicate constellation deal that the YC128 conquest now invalidates. Reading the Caldari conquest as 'restoring' anything requires placing this earlier seizure as the original wound.

nodes.name: Ishukone Wins Intaki Development Rights
nodes.brief: Following the Caldari system auction, Ishukone secured development rights to the Intaki system. Established the Ishukone–Intaki anchoring that Silphy later exploits.
nodes.master_summary: YC111 Caldari system auction outcome. Ishukone won development rights to Intaki, anchoring the Ishukone–Intaki structural relationship that Silphy later inherited and exploited via the Antagonists chronicle.

nodes.name: Death of Otro Gariushi (Quantum Rise summit)
nodes.brief: Caldari–Federation summit attacked: a Nyx mothership collided with the station, framed as Alexander Noir's act. Station and ship safety protections were disabled — not the work of a normal pilot. Mens Reppola became Ishukone CEO afterward.
nodes.master_summary: The killing of Otro Gariushi at the YC110 Caldari–Federation summit, when Alexander Noir's Nyx hit the station with safety protocols disabled. The 'broker' plot targeted Otro because Ishukone was developing Insorum, the Vitoxin antidote that threatened the structural basis of Amarr slavery. Mens Reppola inherited the CEO seat and the Insorum mandate.

nodes.name: Zephyr Ship Distribution (Apocrypha)
nodes.brief: Polymath Valus en Diabel authorized Intaki Syndicate to mass-distribute the Zephyr — a sleeper-ignored exploration ship — to all registered capsuleers. Later revealed to carry covert telemetry/sensor packages funneling wormhole data back to Syndicate (and onward).
nodes.master_summary: The YC111 mass distribution of the Zephyr exploration ship by the Intaki Syndicate. Sleeper-ignored, it allowed capsuleers to map wormhole space — and quietly funneled telemetry data back through the Syndicate to Ishukone. The Zephyr is the original instance of Syndicate-as-data-conduit, structurally identical to the data flows that later sit inside Upwell.

nodes.name: Innerbus → Evermore Transition
nodes.brief: Innerbus chief had a fatal heart attack mid-Yulai dispute (Stellar Transmuters era, Amarr-aligned). Within days, the son installed a new leader who restructured the corp into Evermore and unveiled a pre-prepared AI assistant. Ash flags this as Amarr-targeting shenanigans.
nodes.master_summary: The corporate restructuring that produced Evermore. Innerbus's Amarr-aligned chief died of a heart attack mid-Stellar-Transmuters dispute; the son installed a new leader who immediately rolled out a pre-prepared AI assistant. Reads as a planned takeover targeting Amarr partnerships — Ducasse's later mediator role at Jita is the payoff.

nodes.name: Ashterothi
nodes.brief: EVE lore content creator. Voice of New Eden show. Has been mapping Lai Dai vs Ishukone conspiracy axis for several weeks. Treats Mountain + Ocean factions as anti-Forest.
nodes.master_summary: The curator-of-source for this entire board. Long-form EVE lore content creator running the Voice of New Eden show; has been mapping the Lai Dai vs Ishukone axis on stream for several weeks. His structural framework — Mountain+Ocean vs Forest, soft-drink-wars, SKIN-as-foreshadowing — is the synthesis layer this board is built to externalize.

nodes.name: Alix Moreau
nodes.brief: Gallente Federation President (YC128–). Autonomist Party. Inaugurated under Jita-gate cloud. Issued amnesty to Syndicate descendants. Hawkish Senate factions concerned about her military inexperience.
nodes.master_summary: New Gallente Federation President. Autonomist Party. Inaugurated under the Jita-gate cloud. Issued amnesty to Syndicate descendants during the conquest, positioning the Federation against Caldari rule. Hawkish Senate factions worry her military inexperience hands the State the upper hand.

nodes.name: Soraya Roden
nodes.brief: Progressive Party candidate, runner-up. Roden Shipyards heir. Sponsored by Quafe per Ash. Hawkish military posture.
nodes.master_summary: Election runner-up. Roden Shipyards heir; Quafe-sponsored; hawkish military posture. Would have been the predictable response to Caldari escalation — Moreau's election denied Lai Dai/Forest the Federation hawk they likely backed.

nodes.name: Lucas Tenzin
nodes.brief: Independent candidate, third place. Federal-unity / diplomacy / disadvantaged-citizens platform.
nodes.master_summary: Independent third-place candidate. Federal-unity / diplomacy / disadvantaged-citizens platform. Politically inert post-election but lore-relevant as the establishment-unity option voters rejected.

nodes.name: Silphy en Diabel
nodes.brief: Intaki Syndicate Chair, governor of Poitot. Former Sister of EVE; expelled after Serpentis collaboration. Estranged ally of Sentona Sarpati. Engineered Pas station famine and Aukron coup to seize power. Detained YC128.05.01.
nodes.master_summary: Daughter of the original Syndicate unifier; expelled from the Sisters of Eve after her Serpentis collaboration. Engineered the Pas station famine to seize control of Poitot, then methodically consolidated power across the Syndicate. Her arrest YC128.05.01 broke the Syndicate's leadership backbone and gave the Caldari the pretext to annex.

nodes.name: Dorne en Diabel
nodes.brief: Original unifying Syndicate leader; Silphy's father. Albino, charismatic. Died in a freak accident before clone transfer was possible.
nodes.master_summary: The original unifying Syndicate leader; Silphy's father. Albino, charismatic, set the Syndicate's autonomous-station structure. Died in a freak accident before clone transfer. His death created the succession vacuum that Silphy filled.

nodes.name: Garyn en Diabel
nodes.brief: Silphy's older brother. Inherited Poitot station after Dorne's death. Politically weak; eventually displaced by Silphy.
nodes.master_summary: Silphy's older brother. Inherited Poitot after Dorne's death but was politically weak; Silphy maneuvered around him to seize control. Now sidelined.

nodes.name: Valus en Diabel
nodes.brief: Intaki polymath who designed the Zephyr as minimalist demonstration. Authorized Syndicate mass distribution YC111.
nodes.master_summary: Intaki polymath who designed the Zephyr as a minimalist demonstration. Authorized Syndicate mass distribution YC111 — the act that turned the Zephyr from art project into intelligence asset.

nodes.name: Sentona Sarpati
nodes.brief: Reverend Mother / SoE CEO. Sister of Salvador Sarpati (Serpentis). Once Silphy's closest ally, now her bitter rival; mockingly calls her 'Queen Silphy of the Syndicate.' Conspicuously absent from current news cycle.
nodes.master_summary: Reverend Mother / SoE CEO and Salvador Sarpati's sister. Once Silphy's closest ally, now her bitter rival; she mockingly calls Silphy 'Queen Silphy of the Syndicate.' Her absence from the YC128 news cycle — given her tie to Silphy — is itself a tell that there's a parallel storyline CCP isn't surfacing yet.

nodes.name: Salvador Sarpati
nodes.brief: Head of Serpentis Corporation. Sentona's brother. Silphy's collaborator during her black-market rise.
nodes.master_summary: Head of Serpentis Corporation. Sentona's brother. Silphy's collaborator during her black-market consolidation. The Sarpati siblings are the structural bridge between Sisters of Eve, Serpentis, and the Syndicate — the soft underbelly of the Federation's drug economy.

nodes.name: Otro Gariushi
nodes.brief: Former Ishukone CEO. Gariushi (Guristas-aligned) pilot. Ousted Akelan in a corruption exposé using Riley Rashemo and the Raven project. Killed at Caldari–Federation summit (Quantum Rise) when a Nyx rammed the station.
nodes.master_summary: Former Ishukone CEO. Gariushi (Guristas-aligned) pilot. Rose to power by exposing Akelan's corruption via the Raven project — Miller and Riley Rashemo helped engineer the takeover. Killed at the YC110 Caldari–Federation summit when Alexander Noir's Nyx struck the station. The 'broker' wanted him dead specifically because Ishukone was developing Insorum.

nodes.name: Mens Reppola
nodes.brief: Ishukone CEO since Otro's death. Ash's stream centers him as Ishukone-axis anchor. Working with Mordu's Legion (Muryia Mordu) on clone-soldier development. Heavily drone-protected; in semi-hiding by Templar 1's end.
nodes.master_summary: Ishukone CEO since Otro's death. The structural anchor of the Ishukone axis on Ash's whiteboard. Working with Mordu's Legion (Muryia Mordu) on clone-soldier development; in semi-hiding behind heavy drone protection by Templar 1's end. Every major Ishukone-axis claim about the YC128 cycle eventually routes through him.

nodes.name: Miller Reppola
nodes.brief: Mens's sister. Ishukone CFO. Helped Otro engineer Akelan's removal via blackmail of Riley Rashemo.
nodes.master_summary: Mens's sister, Ishukone CFO. Helped Otro engineer Akelan's removal via blackmail of Riley Rashemo. Operates as Mens's right hand during the post-summit reconstruction.

nodes.name: Raleigh
nodes.brief: Top Ishukone engineer. Built the android Vilamo. Developing cybernetic implant tech (hippocampus / thalamus replacement) for Templar prototypes.
nodes.master_summary: Ishukone's top engineer. Built the android Vilamo. Developing cybernetic implant tech (hippocampus / thalamus replacement) for Templar prototypes. The technical engine behind Ishukone's clone-soldier program.

nodes.name: Gable (codename: Lifegiver)
nodes.brief: Caldari-born scientist; converted to Amarrian faith. Treats the Templar prototype — the only patient with full hippocampus / thalamus cybernetic replacement. Ash identifies her as the 'Lifegiver' figure surfacing in YC128 events.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari-born scientist; converted to Amarrian faith. Treats the Templar prototype — the only patient with full hippocampus / thalamus cybernetic replacement. Ash identifies her as the 'Lifegiver' figure surfacing in YC128 events. Her religious conversion makes her a structural bridge between Ishukone clone-tech and Amarr/Khanid theological politics.

nodes.name: Mr. Lacante
nodes.brief: Tattooed contact in Silphy's Antagonists chronicle. Likely Thukker. Coordinates the Zephyr telemetry data trade with Ishukone (the 'token Ishukone won't be able to resist').
nodes.master_summary: Likely Thukker (facial tattoos). In Silphy's Antagonists chronicle he coordinates the Zephyr telemetry-data trade with Ishukone — 'the kind of token Ishukone won't be able to resist.' The mechanism by which Syndicate intelligence gets monetized via the Mountain/Ocean axis.

nodes.name: Akimaka Saraki
nodes.brief: Chairman of CEP. Mountain Faction. Speaks for Caldari mega-corp consensus through the Warpath crisis. Pledged Caldari 'protection' of Syndicate Intaki against Syndicate leadership itself. Allied with Ocean faction against Forest.
nodes.master_summary: CEP Chairman; Mountain Faction. Public face of the Caldari response across the Warpath cycle — pledged 'protection' of Syndicate Intaki against Syndicate leadership itself. Allied with Ocean against Forest in Ash's framework, which makes every action he takes structurally anti-Lai-Dai.

nodes.name: Haikoriggo
nodes.brief: Caldari Navy Executive General. Led Syndicate operation. Spoke from Intaki Syndicate Bureau on Poitot.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari Navy Executive General who led the Syndicate operation. Spoke from the Intaki Syndicate Bureau on Poitot. Operational counterpart to Saraki's political role.

nodes.name: Alexander Ducasse
nodes.brief: Evermore President. Mediator in the Caldari–Jita-resident negotiations. Controls IMN media network. Per Ash, his rise to power and Evermore's restructuring suggest pre-prepared takeover targeting Amarr.
nodes.master_summary: Evermore President. Mediator in the Caldari–Jita-resident negotiations. Controls IMN media. The Innerbus → Evermore restructuring that put him in power reads as a planned takeover targeting Amarr partnerships, and his current alliance with Saraki produces an IMN coverage pattern that targets Forest-faction Jita managers.

nodes.name: Empress Catiz I
nodes.brief: Amarr Empress. Cast blame on Republic during Warpath escalation. SKIN 'Mandate of Faith' attributed to her call.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr Empress. Took the throne after Jamyl's drifter-assassination. Cast blame on the Republic during Warpath escalation. The 'Mandate of Faith' SKIN attributed to her call positions her as the figurehead of an emerging Amarr-side Mil-Campaign #2.

nodes.name: Celestis Aguard
nodes.brief: Stargate Trailblazer figure. Built Kenninck–Egghelende stargate now serving as the only contiguous Gallente connection after Syndicate annexation.
nodes.master_summary: Stargate Trailblazer figure who built the Kenninck–Egghelende stargate. After the Caldari conquest of Syndicate, his stargate is the only contiguous Gallente connection — without him, Federation space splits and the State has structural transit leverage.

nodes.name: Rio Talifo
nodes.brief: Founder/patron of Anoyus Mapping Project. Has a character seeded in every wormhole system. Public face of the warning that Drifter-tech experimentation could destabilize known space.
nodes.master_summary: AMP founder. Has a character seeded in every wormhole system. Public face of the Drifter-tech risk warning. Vindicated repeatedly by Jita-gate, the prismatic sites, and the Hollow Clone Disaster.

nodes.name: Anar Abdul
nodes.brief: Thukker chief who echoed AMP's concerns over Republic-backed experimentation in liberated Barbican. First cross-empire validation of the Drifter-tech risk warning.
nodes.master_summary: Thukker chief who echoed AMP's concerns over Republic-backed experimentation in liberated Barbican. First cross-empire validation of the Drifter-tech risk warning — and a structural reminder that the Thukker speak independently of the Republic.

nodes.name: Ukumi Pol
nodes.brief: Brutor Chief. Pointed fingers at Amarr post-Jita-gate, invoking Tuvan as historical precedent for reckless tech abuse.
nodes.master_summary: Brutor Chief. Pointed fingers at Amarr post-Jita-gate, invoking Tuvan as historical precedent for reckless tech abuse. Demonstrates Republic tribal-council pluralism: the Sanmatar's neutral statement was followed by tribal chiefs naming names.

nodes.name: Caldari State
nodes.brief: Eight mega-corp federation. Annexed Syndicate YC128.05.05.
nodes.master_summary: Eight-mega-corp federation. Annexed Syndicate YC128.05.05 in the largest forcible nullsec acquisition in modern lore. Its internal politics — Mountain + Ocean vs Forest — drive every action of the Warpath cycle once you read past the empire-vs-empire surface.

nodes.name: Gallente Federation
nodes.brief: Moreau presidency. Disputed Caldari accusations re: Jita-gate.
nodes.master_summary: Moreau presidency, post-Roden defeat. Disputed the Caldari Jita-gate accusations and offered amnesty to Syndicate descendants during the conquest. Structurally weakened by Moreau's inexperience at the worst possible moment.

nodes.name: Minmatar Republic
nodes.brief: Ascendant militarily YC128. Rejected Jita-gate involvement; tribal council co-signed Shakor statement.
nodes.master_summary: Ascendant militarily across YC128. Tribal Council pluralism (Sanmatar Shakur diplomatic, Brutor Chief naming Amarr) gives it more political surface than the other empires. Restored to seven tribes after the Imperian Age.

nodes.name: Amarr Empire
nodes.brief: Catiz I throne. Cast blame on Republic during Warpath cycle.
nodes.master_summary: Catiz I throne. Cast blame on the Republic during Warpath. The 'Mandate of Faith' SKIN suggests Catiz personally is the rallying figure for an emerging Amarr-side Mil-Campaign in the Ammatar Mandate.

nodes.name: Intaki Syndicate
nodes.brief: Loose autonomous federation in Syndicate region. Founded by Caldari-sympathetic Intaki exiles after Caldari Rebellion. Each station autonomous; non-political officially.
nodes.master_summary: Loose autonomous federation in the Syndicate region. Founded by Caldari-sympathetic Intaki exiles after the Caldari Rebellion; each station autonomous; non-political officially. The conquest YC128.05.05 ended its sovereignty but not its membership network — which now operates as a fugitive financial / smuggling structure with capital ships escaping into highsec.

nodes.name: Ishukone Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp. Mountain/Ocean faction-aligned. Original developer of subcranial nano controllers. Acquired Intaki development rights YC111. Deeply involved in clone-soldier / Templar program through Mens Reppola.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari mega-corp, Mountain/Ocean-axis anchor. Original developer of subcranial nano controllers. Acquired Intaki development rights YC111. Co-developed Insorum (Vitoxin antidote) — the act that put a target on Otro Gariushi's back. Currently operating clone-soldier program through Mens Reppola, Raleigh, and Gable. Every Mountain/Ocean win in the Warpath cycle ultimately routes back to Ishukone revenue.

nodes.name: Lai Dai Corporation
nodes.brief: Caldari mega-corp. Forest-faction-aligned. Major beneficiary of Jita IV-4 renovations and Hypernet network. Stole Intaki mind-transfer tech ~YC119. Holds Khanid Amarr ties.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari mega-corp, Forest-axis anchor. Major beneficiary of Jita IV-4 renovations and the Hypernet network — both of which the Warpath restrictions on Jita have just severely impaired. Stole Intaki mind-transfer tech ~YC119. Holds Khanid Amarr ties. The structural loser of the YC128 cycle so far.

nodes.name: Quafe Company
nodes.brief: Beverage corp. Originally Gallente. Strong Lai Dai contracts via Jita IV-4. Sponsored Roden in election. Major beneficiary of Syndicate annexation — new Quafe stations among first structures built in seized space.
nodes.master_summary: Beverage corp. Originally Gallente; now operates strong Lai Dai contracts via Jita IV-4. Sponsored Roden in the election. Major beneficiary of Syndicate annexation — new Quafe stations are among the first structures built in seized space. Allegedly ran nanocontroller-laced soft drinks to influence Gallente senatorial elections (Ash self-flagged tinfoil).

nodes.name: Ytiri
nodes.brief: Ninth-largest Caldari corp. Absorbed by Heth's Provost alliance. Founded Star Seed. Concession statement praised Otro Gariushi — places Ytiri in Ishukone axis.
nodes.master_summary: Ninth-largest Caldari corp. Absorbed by Heth's Provost alliance. Founded Star Seed via its investment division. Concession statement praised Otro Gariushi explicitly — places Ytiri in the Ishukone axis structurally and gives Star Seed its Mountain/Ocean polarity.

nodes.name: Star Seed Drink Co.
nodes.brief: Soft-drink corp founded by Ytiri investment division. 'Official soft drink' of Caldari space; campaigned to remove Quafe ads. Centerpiece of Ash's 'Soft Drink Wars' framing.
nodes.master_summary: Beverage corp founded by Ytiri. 'Official soft drink' of Caldari space; ran ad-removal campaigns against Quafe in YC106. The Ishukone-axis answer to Quafe and the centerpiece of the 'Soft Drink Wars' framing.

nodes.name: Evermore
nodes.brief: Successor to Innerbus. President Alexander Ducasse. Operates Paragon Corporation (only non-Caldari station in Jita) and IMN media network. Pre-prepared corporate-AI rollout suggests engineered transition.
nodes.master_summary: Successor to Innerbus. President Alexander Ducasse. Operates Paragon Corporation (only non-Caldari station in Jita) and IMN media. The pre-prepared corporate-AI rollout at restructuring suggests engineered transition; current alliance with Saraki suggests Mountain/Ocean coordination targeting Lai Dai's Jita revenue.

nodes.name: Innerbus (defunct)
nodes.brief: Predecessor to Evermore. Amarr-aligned. Old leader died of heart attack mid-Stellar Transmuters dispute over Yulai rescue efforts.
nodes.master_summary: Predecessor to Evermore. Amarr-aligned. Old leader died of heart attack mid-Stellar Transmuters dispute over Yulai rescue efforts. The transition out of Innerbus is the Amarr-targeting financial maneuver Ash flags as conspiracy-shaped.

nodes.name: Paragon Corporation
nodes.brief: Evermore subsidiary. Operates the only non-Caldari station in Jita — the structural lever for Ducasse's mediator role.
nodes.master_summary: Evermore subsidiary. Operates the only non-Caldari station in Jita — the structural lever that lets Ducasse mediate between Saraki and Jita residents during the post-Jita-gate restrictions.

nodes.name: IMN — Interstellar Media Network
nodes.brief: Evermore-controlled media. Ash flags its post-Jita-gate coverage as targeting Forest-faction (Lai Dai) Jita managers.
nodes.master_summary: Interstellar Media Network. Evermore-controlled. Its post-Jita-gate coverage targets Forest-faction (Lai Dai) Jita managers, advancing the Mountain/Ocean narrative under cover of impartial reporting.

nodes.name: CONCORD
nodes.brief: Inter-empire peacekeeping authority. Warpath Protocol failed YC128.04.30 — first targeted system breach since Yulai YC110.
nodes.master_summary: Inter-empire peacekeeping authority. Warpath Protocol failed YC128.04.30 — first targeted system breach since Yulai YC110. Funding shifted toward AIR Labs (Ishukone-built) and away from EDENCOM (Upwell-built) reflects the same Mountain/Ocean realignment.

nodes.name: CONCORD Inner Circle
nodes.brief: Inter-empire diplomatic body. Aras Angire heads. Emergency session post-Jita-gate ended early as parties walked out.
nodes.master_summary: CONCORD diplomatic body. Aras Angire heads. Emergency session post-Jita-gate ended early as parties walked out. Its failure during Warpath is the structural reason CONCORD is now reduced to delayed-arrival enforcement.

nodes.name: Chief Executive Panel
nodes.brief: Caldari governing body composed of mega-corp CEOs. Saraki chairs.
nodes.master_summary: Chief Executive Panel. Caldari governing body of the eight mega-corp CEOs. Saraki chairs. Closed-door session post-Jita-gate produced the warmongering posture that drove the Syndicate conquest two weeks later.

nodes.name: Upwell Consortium
nodes.brief: Citadel-tech consortium. Includes Intaki Bank. Builds EDENCOM ships. Forest-faction / Lai Dai-anchored. Financial exposure to Syndicate collapse is the under-discussed angle per Ash.
nodes.master_summary: Citadel-tech consortium. Includes Intaki Bank. Builds EDENCOM ships. Forest-faction / Lai Dai-anchored. Financial exposure to the Syndicate collapse cascades through Intaki Bank into Upwell — an angle CCP coverage has not surfaced and Ash is hammering on stream.

nodes.name: Intaki Bank
nodes.brief: Upwell-affiliated. Subsidiary financial entity tied to Syndicate. Counter-insurgency posture vs Deathless. Now structurally compromised.
nodes.master_summary: Subsidiary financial entity tied to the Syndicate. Upwell-affiliated. Counter-insurgency posture vs Deathless. Now structurally compromised by Silphy's arrest and the Syndicate annexation.

nodes.name: AIR Laboratories
nodes.brief: Ishukone-built research project. Major CONCORD funding shifted to AIR around the same time — a Mountain/Ocean win against Forest. Former SoE member among AIR leadership.
nodes.master_summary: Ishukone-built research project. Major CONCORD funding shifted to AIR around the same time — a Mountain/Ocean win against Forest. A former Sister of Eve sits on its leadership; ties to SoE wormhole research are structural, not coincidental.

nodes.name: EDENCOM
nodes.brief: Inter-empire defensive coalition. Ships built by Upwell. Drifter wormhole occupier (one of five). Partnered with Upwell on Sentinel-MZ research.
nodes.master_summary: Inter-empire defensive coalition. Ships built by Upwell. Drifter wormhole occupier (one of five). Partnered with Upwell on Sentinel-MZ research. Lost CONCORD funding to AIR Labs in the Mountain/Ocean realignment.

nodes.name: Mordu's Legion
nodes.brief: Mercenary group. Muryia Mordu leadership. Co-developer of clone-soldier / Templar tech with Ishukone. Granted full military access to Intaki under YC110-era assembly deal.
nodes.master_summary: Mercenary group under Muryia Mordu. Co-developer of clone-soldier / Templar tech with Ishukone. Granted full military access to Intaki under the YC110-era assembly deal — the legal cover that lets Ishukone operate inside Federation space.

nodes.name: Sisters of EVE
nodes.brief: Religious humanitarian order; Eve Gate research. Two branches: humanitarian and secret-research. Long-standing Drifter / wormhole research. Sentona Sarpati CEO.
nodes.master_summary: Religious humanitarian order; Eve Gate research. Sentona Sarpati CEO. Long-running Drifter and wormhole research program. The branch the public sees is humanitarian; the secret-research branch is one of the few groups in New Eden that's been mapping Drifter tech since before the Crisis.

nodes.name: Serpentis Corporation
nodes.brief: Salvador Sarpati. Black-market / drug pipeline. Silphy's collaborator pre-arrest from SoE.
nodes.master_summary: Salvador Sarpati's black-market / drug pipeline. Silphy's collaborator pre-arrest from SoE. Structural counterpart to the Blood Raiders for the southwestern New Eden map.

nodes.name: Anoyus Mapping Project (AMP)
nodes.brief: Capsuleer initiative working with Gallente Federation. Continuous monitoring of every system in wormhole space. Founded by Rio Talifo. First public sounding on Drifter-tech risks.
nodes.master_summary: Anoyus Mapping Project. Capsuleer initiative working with the Federation. Continuous monitoring of every system in wormhole space. Founded by Rio Talifo. First public sounding on Drifter-tech risks; vindicated by Jita-gate.

nodes.name: Starkmanir Tribe
nodes.brief: Restored Minmatar tribe. Hidden by Nefantar / Ammatar through the Old Wars. Subject of new 'Starkmanir Strategic Wing' SKIN.
nodes.master_summary: Restored Minmatar tribe. Hidden by Nefantar / Ammatar through the Old Wars. Subject of the new 'Starkmanir Strategic Wing' SKIN that pairs with 'Mandate of Faith' to tea-leaf the Mil-Campaign #2 zone.

nodes.name: Ammatar Mandate
nodes.brief: Nefantar-administered Amarr buffer state between Amarr and Minmatar highsec. FW-relevant low/highsec mix. Subject of new 'Mandate of Faith' SKIN. Ash's predicted Mil-Campaign #2 zone.
nodes.master_summary: Nefantar-administered Amarr buffer state between Amarr and Minmatar highsec. Mil-Campaign #2 candidate zone per Ash. The Mandate's existence depends on Damyas III's honorific and is structurally always one historical reveal away from being unmade.

nodes.name: Mountain Faction (Caldari political)
nodes.brief: One of three Caldari sociopolitical factions. Saraki's home faction. Allied with Ocean against Forest in Ash's framework. Ishukone-axis.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari political faction. Saraki's home faction. Allied with Ocean against Forest. Maps onto Ishukone-axis on Ash's whiteboard. The structural winner of the YC128 cycle so far — Saraki's posture, the Syndicate annexation, and the Jita restrictions all benefit Mountain.

nodes.name: Ocean Faction (Caldari political)
nodes.brief: One of three Caldari sociopolitical factions. Mountain ally. Ishukone-axis.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari political faction. Mountain ally. Ishukone-axis. The less-visible half of the dominant alliance — its quiet support is what makes Mountain's actions politically sustainable.

nodes.name: Forest Faction (Caldari political)
nodes.brief: One of three Caldari sociopolitical factions. Lai Dai-axis. Targeted by Mountain/Ocean alignment. Manages Jita-44 / Hypernet revenue per Ash.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari political faction. Lai Dai-axis. Targeted by the Mountain+Ocean alliance. Manages Jita-44 / Hypernet revenue per Ash; the Warpath restrictions on Jita are a direct attack on its income base.

nodes.name: Thukker Tribe
nodes.brief: Most distant Minmatar tribe. Operates in Great Wildlands. Wormhole-interested. Some clans defected to Deathless. Anar Abdul leads. Treated as Minmatar-side equivalent of Intaki Syndicate.
nodes.master_summary: Most distant Minmatar tribe. Operates in Great Wildlands. Wormhole-interested. Some clans defected to Deathless. Anar Abdul leads. Treated as Minmatar-side equivalent of the Intaki Syndicate — independent, monetized, and structurally hard to govern.

nodes.name: Guristas Pirates
nodes.brief: Pirate faction. Historically used by Ishukone to expose Lai Dai corruption. Ash's lead candidate for Warpath message origin ('toys are meant to be played with').
nodes.master_summary: Pirate faction. Historically used by Ishukone to expose Lai Dai corruption. Ash's lead candidate for the Warpath message origin — the 'toys are meant to be played with' tone reads as Guristas-flavored.

nodes.name: Deathless Circle
nodes.brief: Air-aligned. Absorbing Archbine; operating in Pochven. Counter-target of Intaki Bank operations.
nodes.master_summary: Air-aligned. Absorbing Archbine; operating in Pochven. Counter-target of Intaki Bank operations. The faction the Drifter-Crisis-and-after lore arcs are converging toward.

nodes.name: Syndicate Region
nodes.brief: Formerly NPC-nullsec. Caldari sovereign post-YC128.05.05. Strategic value: only NPC-nullsec adjacent to Gallente highsec via PFTAC.
nodes.master_summary: Formerly NPC-nullsec. Caldari sovereign post-YC128.05.05. Strategic value: only NPC-nullsec adjacent to Gallente highsec via PFTAC. Annexation severs Federation contiguity unless Aguard's stargate holds.

nodes.name: Poitot
nodes.brief: Syndicate's de-facto capital. Only named Syndicate system. Site of Silphy's seat and Caldari operation announcement.
nodes.master_summary: Syndicate's de-facto capital. Only named Syndicate system. Site of Silphy's seat — Haikoriggo announced the operation from the Intaki Syndicate Bureau here. The symbolic and operational center of the conquest.

nodes.name: Jita
nodes.brief: New Eden's primary trade hub. All seven gates damaged YC128.04.21. Resident-identity survey: most identify as 'Jita first, Caldari second' — atypical within State.
nodes.master_summary: New Eden's primary trade hub. All seven gates damaged YC128.04.21. Resident-identity survey: most identify as 'Jita first, Caldari second' — atypical within the State. Structurally too important to fail; structurally now political dynamite.

nodes.name: Jita IV-4 (Caldari Navy Assembly Plant)
nodes.brief: Lai Dai-managed renovation cash-cow station. Hypernet hub. Forest-faction revenue centerpiece. Quafe contracts here.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari Navy Assembly Plant moon. Lai Dai-managed renovation cash-cow. Hypernet hub. Forest-faction revenue centerpiece. Quafe contracts here. The financial node Mountain/Ocean is structurally trying to disrupt.

nodes.name: Solitude (region)
nodes.brief: Gallente highsec island. Now reachable only via Kenninck–Egghelende stargate (Aguard's project).
nodes.master_summary: Gallente highsec island. Now reachable only via Aguard's Kenninck–Egghelende stargate. Strategic exposure: if that stargate is destabilized, Solitude detaches.

nodes.name: PFTAC
nodes.brief: Nullsec-highsec adjacency point. Critical breakout-event ingress for Thursday Pelille / Bayet / Orvolle constellations.
nodes.master_summary: Nullsec-highsec adjacency point. Critical breakout-event ingress for Thursday's Pelille / Bayet / Orvolle constellations. Historically the most dangerous nullsec system because it's adjacent to highsec.

nodes.name: Drifter Hyperspace Network
nodes.brief: Drifter-origin data-transfer / hyperspace infrastructure. Manipulated for Jita-gate attack. Access only through wormhole-occupier experimentation. Five occupiers: Amarr, Caldari, EDENCOM, Minmatar, Triglavians.
nodes.master_summary: Drifter-origin data-transfer / hyperspace infrastructure. Manipulated for the Jita-gate attack. Access only via the five wormhole occupiers — the access list is what forces the Caldari Jita-gate accusation against Gallente outward toward a third-party co-conspirator.

nodes.name: Highsec Cyno Beacon Technology
nodes.brief: Illegal/captured cyno tech bypassing empire jamming. Canon since YC123 dread assaults. Re-weaponized for Syndicate breakout.
nodes.master_summary: Illegal/captured cyno-beacon tech that bypasses empire jamming. Canon since the YC123 dread assaults at Tanu and Junfaa. Re-weaponized for the YC128 Syndicate breakout — the single load-bearing piece of inheritance between the Old-Wars-era and the Warpath arc.

nodes.name: Prismatic / Fractured Sites
nodes.brief: New sites containing destroyed Drifter-structure wrecks (wormhole-generator wreckage matches Pochven hardware). Confirms Drifter origin of Catalyst-era anomalies.
nodes.master_summary: New Catalyst-era sites containing destroyed Drifter-structure wrecks. Wreck inventory matches Pochven-zone Drifter wormhole-generator hardware. Structural confirmation that occupier-faction Drifter testing is producing fallout in known space.

nodes.name: Hollow Clone Disaster
nodes.brief: Cloning failure cascade emerging during Crimson Harvest. Ash's theory: caused by Drifter-tech meddling. Order of St. Tetrimon now performing cleansing burns.
nodes.master_summary: Cloning failure cascade emerging during Crimson Harvest. Ash's theory: caused by Drifter-tech meddling. Order of St. Tetrimon now performing cleansing burns of failed clones. The biological consequence of the destructive-testing era.

nodes.name: Soft Drink Wars
nodes.brief: Ash's framing: Quafe (Lai Dai) vs Star Seed (Ishukone). Allegations include nanocontroller-laced soft drinks influencing Gallente senatorial elections. Self-flagged 'tinfoil'.
nodes.master_summary: Ash's framing: Quafe (Lai Dai) vs Star Seed (Ishukone). Allegations include nanocontroller-laced soft drinks influencing Gallente senatorial elections. Self-flagged tinfoil but underwritten by repeated Quafe-Lai-Dai and Star-Seed-Ishukone contractual links.

nodes.name: Lai Dai vs Ishukone Axis
nodes.brief: Ash's whiteboard: durable rivalry running through 20+ years of Caldari political realignment. Mountain+Ocean faction cluster on Ishukone side; Forest on Lai Dai side. Subordinate alignments: SoE / Mountain / Mordu's Legion / Ytiri / Star Seed (Ishukone) vs NOH / CBD / Forest / Quafe / Sarpati (Lai Dai).
nodes.master_summary: The whiteboard. Durable rivalry running through 20+ years of Caldari political realignment. Mountain+Ocean cluster on Ishukone side; Forest on Lai Dai side. SoE / Mordu's Legion / Ytiri / Star Seed map to Ishukone; NOH / CBD / Forest / Quafe / Sarpati line maps to Lai Dai. Without this frame the Warpath beats look like empire-vs-empire; with it they look like a hostile takeover.

nodes.name: Warpath Message Text
nodes.brief: Verbatim CCP text appearing on all impacted CONCORD terminals: 'Warpath, a gift dear empires. After all, toys are meant to be played with.' Origin unattributed; Ash leads Guristas, rules out Deathless / EOM / Sansha.
nodes.master_summary: The CCP text appearing on impacted CONCORD terminals at the moment of attack. Tone is playful-malicious. Origin unattributed; Ash leans Guristas, rules out Deathless / EOM / Sansha. Functions as the only direct creator-inserted clue to Warpath's authorship.

nodes.name: Military Campaigns (YC128 expansion feature)
nodes.brief: Upcoming Faction Warfare expansion mechanic. Ash predicts first campaign = Fate of Syndicate (Caldari hold vs Gallente liberation); second = Ammatar Mandate (Starkmanir vs Nefantar).
nodes.master_summary: Upcoming Faction Warfare expansion mechanic announced for the YC128 expansion cycle. Predicted Mil-Campaign #1: Fate of Syndicate. Predicted Mil-Campaign #2: Ammatar Mandate, with a Starkmanir vs Nefantar tribal axis.

nodes.name: Zephyr Exploration Ship
nodes.brief: Solar-sail propulsion, sleeper-ignored. Distributed YC111 by Syndicate to all capsuleers. Carried covert telemetry/sensor packages funneling wormhole data to Ishukone via the Antagonists pipeline.
nodes.master_summary: Solar-sail propulsion exploration ship. Sleeper-ignored. Distributed YC111 by the Syndicate to all capsuleers. Carried covert telemetry/sensor packages funneling wormhole data into Ishukone via the Antagonists-chronicle pipeline. The original instance of capsuleers being unwitting data-collection agents.

nodes.name: Subcranial Nano Controllers
nodes.brief: Original Ishukone mind-control tech. Banned and replaced — but Ishukone proceeded to bulk-sell trans-cranial microcontrollers to Amarr holders YC111 immediately before Intaki dev-rights win.
nodes.master_summary: Original Ishukone mind-control tech. Banned and replaced — but Ishukone proceeded to bulk-sell trans-cranial microcontrollers to Amarr holders YC111 immediately before winning Intaki dev-rights. The product line that anchors Ishukone's political-economic leverage over the Amarr.

nodes.name: SKIN: State Regulator (SYN-0041)
nodes.brief: Caldari SKIN tagged 'SYN-0041'. Unexplained Syndicate marker. Ash reads as Mil-Campaign zone tag.
nodes.master_summary: Caldari SKIN tagged 'SYN-0041'. Unexplained Syndicate marker on a State skin. Ash reads as Mil-Campaign zone tag. Tinfoil but structurally consistent with the Mountain/Ocean victory framing.

nodes.name: SKIN: President's Decree
nodes.brief: Gallente SKIN. Special agents under direct presidential autonomy. Ties Moreau personally to anti-Caldari ops.
nodes.master_summary: Gallente SKIN. Special agents under direct presidential autonomy. Ties Moreau personally to anti-Caldari ops. Most directly-decoded of the four campaign SKINs.

nodes.name: SKIN: Mandate of Faith
nodes.brief: Amarr SKIN. Empress Catiz I calls; Ammatar Mandate answers. Reads as Amarr-side Mil-Campaign zone.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr SKIN. Empress Catiz I calls; Ammatar Mandate answers. Reads as Amarr-side Mil-Campaign zone. Tinfoil but pairs with Starkmanir Strategic Wing to form a coherent campaign-axis prediction.

nodes.name: SKIN: Starkmanir Strategic Wing
nodes.brief: Minmatar SKIN. Starkmanir tribal-strategy framing. Pairs with Mandate-of-Faith to suggest Ammatar/Starkmanir vs Nefantar campaign axis.
nodes.master_summary: Minmatar SKIN. Starkmanir tribal-strategy framing. Pairs with Mandate of Faith to suggest the next Mil-Campaign will rerun the Imperian Age conflict over the Mandate, but as a player-driven war.

nodes.name: Drone Graviton Emitter
nodes.brief: Item from Catalyst-era drone event. Drops from sentient rogue drones / highsec drone sites; turn-in for Consortium tractor blueprints. Ash's theory: ties to needle-jack filament technology potentially developed by rogue drones.
nodes.master_summary: Item from the Catalyst-era drone event. Drops from sentient rogue drones; turn-in for Consortium tractor blueprints. Ash's theory: ties to needle-jack filament technology potentially developed by rogue drones — graviton manipulation is the underlying physics of all Eve spacetime tech.

nodes.name: Day of Darkness
nodes.brief: Amarr first arrival at Matar. Mythologized dust cloud darkened the sun. Amarr destroyed Matari space infrastructure and began centuries of enslavement.
nodes.master_summary: The Amarr first-contact-and-conquest of Matar. Mythologized as a literal darkening of the sun; canonically the destruction of Matari space infrastructure followed by centuries of slavery. Origin point for every downstream Mimitar–Amarr conflict including the YC128 Warpath cross-incursions.

nodes.name: Bombardment of Starkmanir Prime
nodes.brief: Idonis Ardishapur ordered sustained Tachyon siege-laser orbital bombardment of Starkmanir Prime in retaliation for the killing of Archon Ardishapur. Tribe believed annihilated for centuries.
nodes.master_summary: Idonis Ardishapur's Tachyon orbital bombardment of Starkmanir Prime in retaliation for his father's death. Eradicated the largest Mimitar tribe — apparently. The genocide lit a 250-year fuse to the Great Rebellion and is the founding grievance of the modern Republic.

nodes.name: Mimitar Great Rebellion
nodes.brief: ~250 years after Starkmanir genocide. Slave seizure of a Khoumak became the iconic moment. Galente assistance enabled formation of the Mimitar Republic.
nodes.master_summary: The 250-year-delayed answer to the Starkmanir genocide. A Mimitar slave's seizure of an Amarr Khoumak became the rebellion's iconic moment; Galente assistance enabled the formation of the Mimitar Republic. Without this event there is no four-empire structure for Warpath to fracture.

nodes.name: Sisters of EVE Reveal Starkmanir Survivors
nodes.brief: SoE publicly announced surviving Starkmanir population on Hzeron in the Ammatar Mandate. Triggered the Elder Fleet rescue.
nodes.master_summary: YC110 Sisters of Eve announcement that Starkmanir survivors were alive on Hzeron in the Ammatar Mandate. Forced the hand of the Nefantar conspirators and triggered the Elder Fleet rescue. The reveal is what bridged the Old Wars to the Imperian Age and made the Ammatar-as-secret-protectors framing canonical.

nodes.name: Sarum Prime Superweapon Strike
nodes.brief: Empress Jamyl I destroyed one Elder Fleet strike group at Sarum Prime using a then-unknown superweapon. Two other strike groups simultaneously rescued the Starkmanir + defecting Nefantar.
nodes.master_summary: Empress Jamyl I deployed her then-secret superweapon to destroy one Elder Fleet strike group at Sarum Prime. The other two strike groups simultaneously rescued Starkmanir and defecting Nefantar from the Mandate. Established Jamyl's superweapon as canon — a precedent that informs the Catalyst-era doomsday site rollout.

nodes.name: Ammatar Mass Defection
nodes.brief: Consul-Governor Anaras Ult enabled mass defection of Ammatar back to Mimitar Republic during Elder Fleet attack. Slave owners and Ammatar Navy defected with their charges.
nodes.master_summary: Anaras Ult enabled mass Ammatar defection back to the Republic during the Elder Fleet operation. Slave owners and Navy alike crossed over with their Starkmanir charges. Demonstrated that the Mandate's Nefantar leadership had been long-game protecting Starkmanir — partial redemption of the Ammatar treachery narrative.

nodes.name: Kantar Yun's CONCORD Hypocrisy Speech
nodes.brief: Mimitar ambassador called out CONCORD for protecting Amarr slavery. Pulled aside afterward and shown the secret Elder Fleet project.
nodes.master_summary: Mimitar ambassador Kantar Yun called out CONCORD publicly for protecting Amarr slavery. After the speech, the Mimitar Elders pulled him aside and revealed the secret Elder Fleet project. The speech is the catalyst that converts him from diplomat to revolutionary fellow-traveler.

nodes.name: Empress Jamyl Murdered by Drifters
nodes.brief: Empress Jamyl I killed in Drifter incursion. Triggered an Amarr championship that ultimately resulted in Catiz I's coronation.
nodes.master_summary: Empress Jamyl I murdered by Drifters mid-incursion. Triggered the Amarr championship that Khanid II lost (forcing him into sheal-sin) and that Catiz I won. Without Jamyl's death there is no Catiz, no current Amarr posture, no 'Mandate of Faith' SKIN flavor invoking her call.

nodes.name: Khanid II Forced to Sheal-Sin
nodes.brief: After failing the Amarr championship a second time, Khanid II was forced to perform the ritual of sheal-sin — the ceremonial self-termination required of failed Amarr championship contenders.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid II — who had previously avoided sheal-sin by defecting to form the Khanid Kingdom — lost the post-Jamyl championship and was finally forced to perform the ritual. Closes the centuries-long Khanid avoidance arc and structurally subordinates the Khanid Kingdom to Catiz's authority.

nodes.name: Dread Attack at Tanu II
nodes.brief: One of the YC123 dread assaults: illegal high-sec cyno beacon enabled dread fleet to land at the Ammatar Mandate's capital. The same tech being weaponized for the YC128 Syndicate breakout.
nodes.master_summary: One of the YC123 dread assaults. Illegal high-sec cyno beacon enabled a dread fleet to land at the Ammatar Mandate's capital planet. The same tech precedent now powers the YC128 Syndicate breakout — the Tanu attack is the structural ancestor of the Catalyst-era doomsday rollout.

nodes.name: Kahah Chemical Attacks (YC120)
nodes.brief: Coordinated weaponized Death Glow + Insorum strike across Kahah III and other Kahah planets. Triggered slave revolts. Khanid response under Allar Chakaide killed 300K–9M people per varying estimates.
nodes.master_summary: YC120 weaponized Death Glow + Insorum strike across the Kahah system. Triggered slave revolts; Khanid response under Allar Chakaide killed somewhere between 300K and 9M people. Hardened the Mountain+Ocean / Forest split inside Caldari and gave Chakaide his anti-Ammatar reputation.

nodes.name: Lost Adama Discovery
nodes.brief: Ida monk Negui Erlon claimed an Intaki slave (Eloa Enaro) aboard the Pure Faith Harvest was a reincarnated Adama who had vanished in YC99. Khanid Sebiros Atsa Kufair refused to surrender her.
nodes.master_summary: Ida monk Negui Erlon claimed a slave aboard the Pure Faith Harvest was a reincarnated Adama who had vanished in YC99. Khanid Sebiros Atsa Kufair refused to surrender her. Sets up the Pure Faith Harvest raid and the Galente sanctions cycle.

nodes.name: Pure Faith Harvest Raid
nodes.brief: Intaki extremists assaulted the Khanid cruiser carrying Enaro near a stargate at Vasala. Repulsed by Royal Khanid Navy. Galente sanctions on Amarr followed.
nodes.master_summary: Intaki extremists assaulted the Khanid cruiser holding Eloa Enaro. Repulsed by the Royal Khanid Navy. Triggered the Federation–Republic sanctions wave against the Empire. A precedent for civilian-religious-figure-as-pretext that echoes through the YC128 cycle.

nodes.name: Skarkon Angel Cartel Takeover
nodes.brief: ~YC110: Angel Cartel took control of Skarkon system; locals voted to join the Cartel and shut their stargates against the Republic. Symbol of Mimitar government weakness in the era.
nodes.master_summary: ~YC110: Angel Cartel took control of the Mimitar system of Skarkon; locals voted to join the Cartel and shut their stargates against the Republic. Symptomatic of Republic governance weakness during the Midular era and a structural parallel for what nullsec NPC factions do when given political opening.

nodes.name: Empress Jamyl I
nodes.brief: Wielded the Sarum Prime superweapon against the Elder Fleet. Later murdered by Drifters. Catiz I succeeded her.
nodes.master_summary: Wielded the Sarum Prime superweapon against the Elder Fleet. Later murdered by Drifters. Catiz I succeeded her. Jamyl's superweapon is canonical proof that empires sit on weapons CONCORD can't constrain — the structural ancestor of the Catalyst-era doomsday site.

nodes.name: Khanid II
nodes.brief: Defected from Amarr ~200+ years ago to avoid sheal-sin after first championship loss; founded Khanid Kingdom. Lost the Jamyl-succession championship, was forced to perform sheal-sin.
nodes.master_summary: Defected from Amarr ~200+ years ago to avoid sheal-sin after his first championship loss; founded the Khanid Kingdom. Lost the post-Jamyl championship and was finally forced to perform the ritual. His arc closes the long-running Khanid-as-rebel-kingdom narrative.

nodes.name: Emperor Damyas III
nodes.brief: Bestowed 'Ammatar' honorific on the Nefantar tribe in recognition of their loyalty. Origin of the mandate's name.
nodes.master_summary: The Amarr emperor who bestowed the 'Ammatar' honorific on the Nefantar tribe in recognition of their loyalty during the Mimitar enslavement era. The naming event that makes the Mandate possible — and the act that frames the Nefantar as both traitor and trusted vassal.

nodes.name: Archon Ardishapur
nodes.brief: Orthodox Amarr royal heir. Replaced liberal holder Arzad Hamri over Starkmanir; killed in Starkmanir uprising.
nodes.master_summary: Orthodox Amarr royal heir. Replaced the liberal Arzad Hamri as holder of Starkmanir. His religious crackdown triggered the Starkmanir uprising; he was killed in it, which provoked his son's bombardment of Starkmanir Prime.

nodes.name: Arzad Hamri
nodes.brief: Liberal Amarr holder of Starkmanir. Tolerated heretical Mimitar interpretation of Amarr faith. Executed by orthodox authorities and replaced by Archon Ardishapur.
nodes.master_summary: Liberal Amarr holder of Starkmanir who tolerated their heretical interpretation of the Amarr faith. Executed by orthodox authorities and replaced by Archon Ardishapur. His removal is the proximate cause of the Starkmanir genocide.

nodes.name: Idonis Ardishapur
nodes.brief: Son of Archon Ardishapur. Ordered the orbital bombardment of Starkmanir Prime in retaliation for his father's death.
nodes.master_summary: Son of Archon Ardishapur. Ordered the orbital bombardment that annihilated Starkmanir Prime. The Ardishapur house remains a major Amarr political bloc named for him.

nodes.name: Anaras Ult
nodes.brief: Ammatar Consul-Governor (YC110). Nefantar conspirator. Encouraged the mass Ammatar defection to the Republic during the Elder Fleet attack.
nodes.master_summary: Ammatar Consul-Governor at the time of the Elder Fleet attack. Nefantar conspirator who actively enabled the mass Ammatar defection back to the Republic. The political face of the Nefantar long-game protection of the Starkmanir.

nodes.name: Kantar Yun
nodes.brief: Mimitar ambassador to CONCORD. Public hypocrisy speech triggered the chain that led the Mimitar Elders to reveal the Elder Fleet project to him.
nodes.master_summary: Mimitar ambassador to CONCORD. His public hypocrisy speech triggered the chain that led the Mimitar Elders to reveal the secret Elder Fleet project to him. Continues to play diplomatic spoiler roles into the YC120 Kahah crisis.

nodes.name: Karin Midular
nodes.brief: Mimitar Sanmatar at the time of the CONCORD protest era. Pursued peace and appeasement with Amarr — controversial within the Republic for sidelining the still-enslaved.
nodes.master_summary: Mimitar Sanmatar of the CONCORD-protest era. Pursued peace and appeasement with Amarr — controversial within the Republic because it sidelined the still-enslaved population. Her track was the structural backdrop the Elders worked around when building the Elder Fleet.

nodes.name: Alika Valkanir
nodes.brief: Chief of restored Nefantar tribe (chosen YC115).
nodes.master_summary: Chief of the restored Nefantar tribe (chosen YC115). Symbolic anchor of the post-Imperian-Age Nefantar reintegration — and the figure any future Mil-Campaign #2 narrative would have to either fold into Republic legitimacy or split off.

nodes.name: Jamal Sarum
nodes.brief: Issued post-Elder-Fleet proclamation welcoming Starkmanir, Thukker, and Nefantar back into the Mimitar Republic.
nodes.master_summary: Issued the post-Elder-Fleet proclamation welcoming Starkmanir, Thukker, and Nefantar back into the Mimitar Republic. The legal-rhetorical act that made the tribal restoration canonical.

nodes.name: Allar Chakaide
nodes.brief: Khanid Sebiros, Plenipotentiary, commander of the 19th Royal Uolans. Led the brutal Kahah pacification YC120; openly hostile to Ammatar and accused Sisters of Eve and Mimitar of orchestrating the chemical attacks.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid Sebiros, Plenipotentiary, commander of the 19th Royal Uolans. Led the brutal YC120 Kahah pacification; openly hostile to the Ammatar; accused Sisters of Eve and Mimitar of orchestrating the chemical attacks. The Mimitar countered that he himself had Blood Raider ties — neither claim has been resolved canonically.

nodes.name: Eloa Enaro
nodes.brief: Intaki slave on the Pure Faith Harvest claimed by the Ida faith to be a reincarnated lost Adama from YC99.
nodes.master_summary: Intaki slave aboard the Pure Faith Harvest claimed by the Ida faith to be a reincarnated lost Adama from YC99. The MacGuffin around which the YC120 Intaki–Khanid extremist cycle pivots.

nodes.name: Negui Erlon
nodes.brief: Ida monk who claimed to discover the Lost Adama in YC120 aboard a Khanid cruiser.
nodes.master_summary: Ida monk who claimed to discover the Lost Adama in YC120 aboard a Khanid cruiser. Catalyst figure — his claim turned a slave purchase dispute into a religious-extremist flashpoint.

nodes.name: Atsa Kufair
nodes.brief: Khanid Sebiros, owner of the Pure Faith Harvest. Refused to sell or surrender Eloa Enaro despite Ida monk's claims.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid Sebiros, owner of the Pure Faith Harvest. Refused to sell or surrender Eloa Enaro. The structural antagonist of the Lost Adama arc and the proximate cause of the Pure Faith Harvest raid.

nodes.name: Maleatu Shakur
nodes.brief: Mimitar Sanmatar during the YC128 Warpath cycle. Co-signed the Republic statement rejecting involvement in the Jita gate incident.
nodes.master_summary: Mimitar Sanmatar during the YC128 Warpath cycle. Co-signed the Republic statement rejecting involvement in the Jita gate incident. The diplomatic counterpart to Brutor Chief Ukumi Pol's tribal accusation against the Amarr.

nodes.name: Tanu II
nodes.brief: Capital planet of the Ammatar Mandate. Site of one of the YC123 dread attacks. Currently hosts a memorial bookmark for Empress Catiz's Honor Guard.
nodes.master_summary: Capital planet of the Ammatar Mandate. Site of one of the YC123 dread attacks — the canonical precedent for the high-sec cyno tech now being reused for the Syndicate breakout. Currently hosts a memorial bookmark for Empress Catiz's Honor Guard.

nodes.name: Hzeron
nodes.brief: Planet within the Ammatar Mandate where the Nefantar hid the surviving Starkmanir population for centuries.
nodes.master_summary: Planet within the Ammatar Mandate where the Nefantar hid surviving Starkmanir for centuries. The geographic point where the Old Wars' biggest secret was kept — and revealed.

nodes.name: Matar
nodes.brief: Mimitar homeworld. Pre-Amarr paradise planet. Weakened by moon-mining-induced ecological imbalance before Amarr arrival.
nodes.master_summary: Mimitar homeworld. Pre-Amarr paradise planet. Weakened by moon-mining-induced ecological imbalance before the Day of Darkness. The origin point of every Minmatar character arc.

nodes.name: Starkmanir Prime
nodes.brief: Former Starkmanir tribal homeworld. Reduced to barren wasteland by orbital Tachyon bombardment ordered by Idonis Ardishapur.
nodes.master_summary: Former Starkmanir tribal homeworld. Reduced to barren wasteland by orbital Tachyon bombardment ordered by Idonis Ardishapur. Symbolic anchor of every Starkmanir-restoration storyline.

nodes.name: Arzad
nodes.brief: Amarr-invaded system where the Starkmanir tribe was first enslaved.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr-invaded system where the Starkmanir tribe was first enslaved. Historic precedent for the Amarr invasion-and-conversion playbook.

nodes.name: Sarum Prime
nodes.brief: Adjacent to Amarr Prime. Site of the Elder Fleet's deepest strike — destroyed by Jamyl I's superweapon.
nodes.master_summary: Adjacent to Amarr Prime. Site of the Elder Fleet's deepest strike — destroyed by Jamyl I's superweapon. The system whose destruction proved Amarr could field weapons CONCORD couldn't constrain.

nodes.name: Kahah System
nodes.brief: Khanid Kingdom system. Site of the YC120 chemical attacks and subsequent mass-casualty pacification.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid Kingdom system. Site of the YC120 chemical attacks and subsequent mass-casualty pacification. The atrocity that hardened the Mimitar–Khanid divide and gave Chakaide his profile.

nodes.name: Skarkon
nodes.brief: Mimitar system that defected to the Angel Cartel ~YC110 — symbol of Republic governance weakness.
nodes.master_summary: Mimitar system that defected to the Angel Cartel ~YC110 — symbol of Republic governance weakness and a cautionary precedent for what happens when an empire stops servicing a region.

nodes.name: Three-Tier Amarr Structure
nodes.brief: Amarr space divided into: Amarr proper, Khanid Kingdom, and Ammatar Mandate. Each has distinct allegiance, governance, and tribal origin.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr space divided into Amarr proper, Khanid Kingdom, and Ammatar Mandate. Each tier has distinct allegiance, governance, and tribal origin. The frame that makes the Mil-Campaign #2 prediction structurally legible — Mandate is the seam where Amarr internal politics is most exposed.

nodes.name: Seven Mimitar Tribes
nodes.brief: Starkmanir, Nefantar (now Ammatar), Brutor, Krusual, Sebiestor, Vherokior, Thukker. Restored to seven after Imperian Age.
nodes.master_summary: Starkmanir, Nefantar (now Ammatar), Brutor, Krusual, Sebiestor, Vherokior, Thukker. Restored to seven after the Imperian Age. The structural unit of Mimitar political identity.

nodes.name: Sheal-Sin
nodes.brief: Amarr ritual of ceremonial self-termination required of failed championship contenders. Khanid II's repeated avoidance of this ritual is a major lore thread.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr ritual of ceremonial self-termination required of failed championship contenders. Khanid II's repeated avoidance of this ritual — first by defection, then by championship retry — defined Khanid Kingdom autonomy until Catiz's coronation forced him into it.

nodes.name: Emergency Militia War Powers Act
nodes.brief: CONCORD act that established Faction Warfare. Cited by Kantar Yun in the YC120 Kahah crisis as having been violated repeatedly in highsec without consequence.
nodes.master_summary: Emergency Militia War Powers Act. CONCORD act that established Faction Warfare. Cited by Kantar Yun in the YC120 Kahah crisis as having been violated repeatedly in highsec without consequence — the precedent CONCORD's Warpath failure now repeats at scale.

nodes.name: Vitoxin Slave-Control Chemistry
nodes.brief: Amarr slave-control compound. Fatally addictive — withdrawal kills. Maintains slave dependence on Amarr supply.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr slave-control compound. Fatally addictive — withdrawal kills. Maintains slave dependence on Amarr supply. The pharmacological backbone of the Amarr economy; disrupting it threatens the entire Khanid agricultural base.

nodes.name: Insorum
nodes.brief: Synthetic Vitoxin antidote co-developed by Ishukone and Galente parties. Threatened the structural basis of Amarr slavery; targeted by 'the broker' plot. Otro Gariushi's death traces to this conflict.
nodes.master_summary: Synthetic Vitoxin antidote co-developed by Ishukone and Galente parties. Threatened the structural basis of Amarr slavery; the broker plot targeted Insorum production and produced Otro Gariushi's death. Insorum's survival is why Mens Reppola operates under heavy drone protection.

nodes.name: Death Glow
nodes.brief: Hallucinogenic illegal booster derived from chronodynamic-tricaroxal. Weaponized in the YC120 Kahah attacks. Linked to Blood Raider supply chains.
nodes.master_summary: Hallucinogenic illegal booster derived from chronodynamic-tricaroxal. Weaponized in the YC120 Kahah attacks. Linked to Blood Raider supply chains — and to the Death Glow Hunters who appear as Crimson Harvest enemies year after year.

nodes.name: Chronodynamic Tricaroxal
nodes.brief: Rare ore reprocessed into both cerebral accelerators (legitimate) and Death Glow (illegal). Same chemistry feeds skill-acceleration boosters and chemical-attack ordinance.
nodes.master_summary: Rare ore reprocessed into both cerebral accelerators (legitimate) and Death Glow (illegal). The dual-use chemistry that lets the same supply chain feed CONCORD-sanctioned skill acceleration and weaponized chemical attacks.

nodes.name: Khanid Kingdom
nodes.brief: Third tier of Amarr space. Defected ~200 years ago under Khanid II. Hosts the brutal 19th Royal Uolans. Strong Lai Dai ties per Ash.
nodes.master_summary: Third tier of Amarr space. Defected ~200 years ago under Khanid II. Hosts the brutal 19th Royal Uolans. Strong Lai Dai ties per Ash. Its tier-three status makes it the political wedge Lai Dai works through inside the Empire.

nodes.name: Mimitar Elders
nodes.brief: Mythological progenitor council of the seven tribes. Believed lost; secretly active. Funded the Elder Fleet through Thukker channels.
nodes.master_summary: Mythological progenitor council of the seven tribes. Believed lost; secretly active. Funded the Elder Fleet through Thukker channels. The organizational shape of the Mimitar deep state.

nodes.name: Elder Fleet
nodes.brief: Three-strike-group Mimitar dreadnought-heavy navy built in secret with Thukker money. One strike group destroyed at Sarum Prime; two rescued Starkmanir + Nefantar from the Mandate.
nodes.master_summary: Three-strike-group Mimitar dreadnought-heavy navy built in secret with Thukker money and Mimitar Elder direction. One strike group destroyed at Sarum Prime; two rescued Starkmanir + Nefantar from the Mandate. The original instance of empire-scale covert military buildup.

nodes.name: Blood Raider Covenant
nodes.brief: Heretical Amarr blood-cult faction. Tied to early Insorum chemical attacks (pre-YC120) and Death Glow harvesting. Allar Chakaide accused them and the Ammatar of conspiring against the Khanid.
nodes.master_summary: Heretical Amarr blood-cult faction. Tied to early Insorum chemical attacks (pre-YC120) and Death Glow harvesting. Allar Chakaide accused them and the Ammatar of conspiring against the Khanid; the Mimitar accused him in return.

nodes.name: Ida Faith / Ida Monastery
nodes.brief: Intaki religion. Believes in reincarnation; Adamas reincarnate without technology. Conflict with Khanid slavers over Lost Adama Eloa Enaro.
nodes.master_summary: Intaki religion. Believes in reincarnation; Adamas reincarnate without technology. The structural source of Intaki political-cultural distinctiveness inside the Federation — and the cause that radicalized Intaki extremists in YC120.

nodes.name: 19th Royal Uolans
nodes.brief: Khanid regiment commanded by Allar Chakaide. Carried out the Kahah pacification; clone facility was target of the YC120 viral strike.
nodes.master_summary: Khanid regiment commanded by Allar Chakaide. Carried out the Kahah pacification; the clone facility holding Chakaide's own clone was target of the YC120 viral strike that started the cycle.

nodes.name: Iron Alliance (capsuleer)
nodes.brief: Capsuleer raider group. Attacked the SoE-Mortis-Legion relief convoy bound for Rashard V (YC108).
nodes.master_summary: Capsuleer raider group. Attacked the SoE-Mordu's Legion relief convoy bound for Rashard V (YC108). Independent enough to act against an SoE convoy, structured enough to coordinate with capital weaponry.

nodes.name: Khoumak (Amarr religious scepter)
nodes.brief: Symbol of Amarr ecclesiastical authority. A Mimitar slave seizing one became the iconic moment of the Great Rebellion.
nodes.master_summary: Amarr religious scepter. A Mimitar slave seizing one became the iconic moment of the Great Rebellion. Symbolic artifact of Amarr ecclesiastical authority and the moment it cracked.

nodes.name: Exordium Charter (Yulai Accord Amendment)
nodes.brief: CONCORD Inner Circle + AIR Laboratories signed an amendment to the Yulai Accords establishing the new region of Exordium with no-violence security protocols. Public ceremony held in orbit around Yulai 8.
nodes.master_summary: The constitutional event for the Exordium region. CCP's first canonical move toward a deconfliction zone — CONCORD and AIR jointly chartered a 50-system region with capsuleer-side safety enforced by Yulai Accord amendment. Connects directly to AIR Labs' rising profile in the Mountain/Ocean realignment, and to the Drifter-experimentation policy thread (the CONCORD-funded research that would otherwise have flowed to EDENCOM).

nodes.name: Exordium
nodes.brief: New region of 50+ star systems linked by newly constructed stargates. Single two-way connection to Yulai (under construction); four one-way shipcasters into each empire's sovereign space via embassy constellations.
nodes.master_summary: Aurora's first canonical neutral space. Strategic significance: the only region in New Eden with capsuleer-violence protocols structurally enforced. Hosts AIR's relocated capsuleer training program, which positions Exordium as the new front door for new pilots — a structural shift away from the Caldari/Ishukone training pipeline that has historically held that role.

nodes.name: Yulai Accords
nodes.brief: Foundational CONCORD treaty that gave rise to faction warfare's emergency war powers framework and now the Exordium Charter amendment.
nodes.master_summary: The constitutional document of CONCORD-era New Eden. Every multi-empire structural framework — EMWPA, Exordium charter, Warpath Protocol — extends or amends the Yulai Accords. Warpath Protocol Failure is, in this reading, a breach of the same accords being amended to create Exordium two weeks earlier.

nodes.name: Aldeya Balasharian
nodes.brief: AIR Laboratories CEO. Public face of the Exordium charter and AIR's super-stable warp drive technology.
nodes.master_summary: AIR's leadership face during the Exordium era. Announced AIR's super-stable warp drive — the technical primitive that made building a new region in years rather than decades possible. Ties AIR Labs to a named principal that connects upward to the Mountain/Ocean realignment Ash mapped on his whiteboard.

nodes.name: Aras Angarae
nodes.brief: Head of CONCORD Inner Circle. Co-signatory of the Exordium Charter; managed the post-Jita-gate Inner Circle emergency session.
nodes.master_summary: The diplomatic face of CONCORD across the YC128 cycle. Signed the Exordium amendment with Balasharian, then chaired the post-Jita-gate session that ended with all parties walking out. Two consecutive multi-empire signatures bracketing the Warpath Protocol Failure.

nodes.name: AIR Super-Stable Warp Drive
nodes.brief: Proprietary AIR technology allowing colony ships to maintain warp between stars. Cut Exordium construction from decades to years.
nodes.master_summary: The technical primitive that made Exordium feasible in human-scale time. Signals AIR Labs has crossed into proprietary deep-tech (vs pure research) — a positioning that puts it on the same axis as Ishukone's Templar/clone program. Likely candidate for the next wave of capsuleer-relevant ship modules.

nodes.name: Capsuleer Training Relocation to Exordium
nodes.brief: AIR-managed training programs (which AIR took over in YC123) relocated to Exordium. New capsuleers will be born in the no-violence region.
nodes.master_summary: Structural shift in the new-pilot pipeline. From day one of their career, capsuleers will be physically in AIR-managed CONCORD-protected space — not the Caldari corp-state milieu. Long-tail consequences: changes the cultural baseline of new pilots, weakens the Caldari training-as-recruitment lever the State has historically exploited.

nodes.name: Eve Gate Collapse
nodes.brief: Wormhole connecting New Eden to Earth/Sol collapsed ~861 AD (in-universe), severing the colonies from humanity's origin and triggering civilizational collapse across the cluster.
nodes.master_summary: The break point between proto-history and modern New Eden. Every modern faction descends from a colony cut off by this event. Sisters of Eve research is fundamentally an attempt to understand and possibly reopen the gate. The Drifters' hyperspace network — the same one weaponized for Jita-gate — is structurally the heir to whatever made the original Eve gate work.

nodes.name: Yan Jung
nodes.brief: Pre-collapse civilization in the Dor region (modern Gallente space). Masters of gravitonic and force-field technology. Records end ~9000 AD.
nodes.master_summary: One of three pre-collapse civilizations. Yan Jung gravitonic technology is the structural ancestor of every stargate, warp drive, and cyno beacon in modern New Eden — including the Drone Graviton Emitter the rogue drones are now manufacturing in the Catalyst-era event. Their disappearance ~9000 AD remains canonically unexplained.

nodes.name: Talocan
nodes.brief: Pre-collapse civilization. Remains found primarily in Anoikis (wormhole space). Fell from power around the same era as the Yan Jung.
nodes.master_summary: Pre-collapse civilization whose remnants seed modern Anoikis. Their collapse coincides with the Yan Jung's — suggesting a common cause that current EVE lore has not surfaced. Talocan structures are the anchor points for modern wormhole-occupier Drifter tech research.

nodes.name: First Jove Empire
nodes.brief: Earliest documented Jove polity. Collapsed when 'elders' suppressed biological experimentation by other Jovians.
nodes.master_summary: First of three Jove empires. Its collapse is the foundational political-philosophy split inside Jove civilization — engineered restraint vs biological license. The same axis returns in the Second Empire (Boore) and informs the Jovian Disease emergence.

nodes.name: Second Jove Empire (Michael Boore)
nodes.brief: Reunified Jove civilization under Michael Boore. Era of intensive bio-experimentation that produced the Jovian Disease and seeded the Sleeper civilization.
nodes.master_summary: The Second Empire's bio-experiment culture is the proximate cause of every modern Drifter / Sleeper / Jovian-Disease beat. Boore reunified Jove after the First Empire's collapse but his enabling of unrestricted experimentation is what produced the engineered defect that still terminates Jovians on a long-enough timeline.

nodes.name: Michael Boore
nodes.brief: Reunifier of the Jove people into the Second Empire after the First Empire's collapse.
nodes.master_summary: Founding figure of Jove modernity. His political project (reunification) succeeded; the bio-experimental license he enabled produced the Jovian Disease that defines the entire Jove arc afterward. Cautionary canonical figure for what unrestricted research does inside a small genetically-tight population.

nodes.name: Jovian Disease
nodes.brief: Engineered biological defect in Jovian DNA. Causes melancholy, madness, and eventual death across all Jovians on a sufficiently long timeline.
nodes.master_summary: The chronic existential crisis of the Jove. Every Jovian arc — turning to the Construct, the Sleeper transition, eventual Drifter emergence — is a response to this defect. Insorum's structural analog: Vitoxin is to the Mimitar what Jovian Disease is to the Jove, except the Jove engineered theirs themselves.

nodes.name: The Construct
nodes.brief: Jovian VR / simulated environment. Used time dilation to advance Jove civilization beyond physical-world limits. Eventually rewired to use connected biological brains as computational substrate.
nodes.master_summary: The Construct is the Jovian deep state — every Jove who matters has spent significant subjective time inside it. The 'one body, one mind' law is the constitutional safeguard around it. The Sleeper civilization is what happens when Jove-bound consciousnesses commit fully to the Construct and never come out. The Drifter emergence is canonically tied to whatever happened to those consciousnesses afterward.

nodes.name: One Body, One Mind Law
nodes.brief: Jovian constitutional rule: a consciousness in the Construct must also have a physical body connected to it.
nodes.master_summary: The structural safeguard against pure-Construct existence. Connecting body to mind kept Jovians tethered to physical reality and locked their brains into the Construct's compute substrate. Violations of this law underwrite most of the canonical Jove failure modes that follow.

nodes.name: The Deathless
nodes.brief: Mysterious figure first surfacing YC110 era. Operates the Deathless Network, distributes intel and resources across pirate factions, currently seen pulling Archbine away from Mordu's Legion / Mu and into his own circle.
nodes.master_summary: The current cycle's antagonist-of-record. Modern Deathless arc converges multiple long-running threads: stolen capsuleer-grade resources, unknown connection to Jove deep history (the name + immortality framing reads as Construct-derived), Pochven operations, Archbine defection. Counter-target of Intaki Bank / Upwell counter-insurgency.

nodes.name: Deathless Network
nodes.brief: Distribution arm of the Deathless. Seeds intel and resources to pirate factions; documented role in distributing capsuleer-grade resources stolen by Esri-class operations to other pirate groups.
nodes.master_summary: The Deathless Network is the operational layer between the Deathless himself and the pirate ecosystem. Functions as a black-market intel exchange — same shape as the Antagonists-chronicle Sentona/Lacante/Ishukone data trade, but with the Deathless rather than Ishukone as the terminal recipient. Where Intaki Bank's counter-insurgency is aimed.
